text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members regularly merge their code changes into a shared main branch. Each time new code is added, a CI pipeline automatically triggers a process. This includes automated builds, where the software is compiled or packaged, and running tests to check if the code works correctly. The main goal of CI is catching issues early, before they become bigger problems in the project. By using CI, developers can find bugs and integration mistakes quickly, fix them more easily, and keep the project stable. This helps teams work faster and with more confidence.
